questions.question: Which state is the host of 49th Yonex Sunrise Junior National Badminton Championship 2026?
questions.explanation: Sikkim has been selected as the host for the 49th Yonex Sunrise Junior National Badminton Championship 2026 (U-19, Individual & Team). The championship is scheduled to be held from 14th to 21st November 2026. The tournament is organised under the aegis of the Badminton Association of India (BAI). It will feature top junior badminton players from across India, competing at a national level.

questions.question: Who won the Women’s Singles title at the Indian Open Squash 2026?
questions.explanation: Anahat Singh won the Women’s Singles title at the Indian Open Squash in Mumbai, retaining her crown. She defeated second seed Egyptian player Hana Moataz 3-1 in the final, dropping only one game in the entire tournament. Abhay Singh won the Men’s Singles title at the same event, defeating fellow Indian Veer Chotrani 3-0 in just 32 minutes. Abhay displayed complete control and superior shot-making to claim a convincing victory in the all-Indian men’s final.

questions.question: Kipa Mero won which medal in Women’s Javelin Throw F12 Category at the 24th National Para Athletics Championships 2026?
questions.explanation: Kipa Mero won the Gold Medal in Women’s Javelin Throw F12 Category at the 24th National Para Athletics Championships 2026 in Bhubaneswar, Odisha. She broke her own National Record, demonstrating dedication, resilience, and sporting excellence. Techi Meta won the Silver Medal in the Women’s 400 metre T13 Category at the same championships.

questions.question: Which state topped the medal tally in Khelo India Tribal Games 2026?
questions.explanation: The Khelo India Tribal Games concluded in Chhattisgarh, with the closing ceremony attended by Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai, highlighting the promotion of tribal sports talent. Renowned athletes Mary Kom and Bhaichung Bhutia participated as special guests, encouraging young players. In the overall medal tally, Karnataka emerged as the champion with 23 gold medals, followed by Odisha (second) and Jharkhand (third). The host state Chhattisgarh finished ninth with 3 gold, 10 silver, and 6 bronze medals. A total of about 3,800 athletes from 30 States and Union Territories participated, reflecting wide national representation.

questions.question: To Lam has been elected as president of which country?
questions.explanation: To Lam was unanimously elected President of Vietnam for a five-year term, while continuing as Communist Party chief. This consolidates power, departing from Vietnam’s tradition of collective leadership and resembling Xi Jinping-style centralisation. He prioritised peace, stability, and economic growth with improved livelihoods. His rise follows an anti-corruption drive as head of the Ministry of Public Security.

questions.question: Palak and Mukesh Nelavalli won which medal in the 10m air pistol mixed team event at the ISSF World Cup 2026?
questions.explanation: Palak and Mukesh Nelavalli won gold medal in the 10m air pistol mixed team event at the ISSF World Cup 2026, Spain. They set a new world record with a combined score of 487.7, also marking a junior world record. The previous record was held by Uzbekistan shooters. In the final, they defeated China (silver) and Hungary (bronze). International Shooting Sport Federation (ISSF) governs the event.

questions.question: The Para Elan initiative has been jointly launched by India and which country?
questions.explanation: The Para Elan initiative is a three-year India–France collaboration launched by the French Institute in India and the Paralympic Committee of India to promote inclusive education and para sports. A Memorandum of Understanding was signed by Satya Prakash Sangwan (PCI) and Gregor Trumel (IFI) in the presence of Thierry Mathou. The programme aims to reach around 10,000 students through awareness campaigns, workshops, and interactions with para-athletes. It focuses on values like inclusion, equality, resilience, and excellence while strengthening India–France cooperation in adaptive sports.

questions.question: Which city is the host of 2027 World Para Athletics Championships?
questions.explanation: World Para Athletics announced that Tashkent will host the 2027 World Para Athletics Championships. It will be the 13th edition and the first time Central Asia hosts the event. The championship will be held at the Olympic City Main Stadium in Tashkent. It is governed by the International Paralympic Committee. The event features top para-athletes from across the world. It serves as a qualification pathway for the Paralympic Games.

questions.question: Which state is the host of 24th National Junior (U20) Athletics Federation Competition 2026?
questions.explanation: Karnataka hosted the 24th National Junior (U20) Athletics Federation Competition 2026. Around 1059 athletes, including 709 male participants, are competing in various track and field events. The event serves as a qualifier for the Asian U20 Athletics Championships to be held in Hong Kong. The competition provides a major platform for young athletes to qualify for international events and represent India.

questions.question: Who won the Under-17 Boys National title at the 87th Inter-State Junior and Youth National Table Tennis Championships?
questions.explanation: Ritvik Gupta from Jammu and Kashmir won the Under-17 Boys National title at the 87th Inter-State Junior and Youth National Table Tennis Championships. The championship was organised under the Table Tennis Federation of India and held in Dehradun. In the final, he defeated Sarthak Arya of Odisha in a close 4–3 match. Ritvik became the first player from Jammu & Kashmir to win a national U-17 boys title in table tennis.
